strengths: Clear identification of three specific, real-world objects with precise historical and cultural contexts
Consistent links between each object and the IA prompt âWho owns knowledge?â using TOK concepts such as epistemic injustice and collective interpretation
Appropriate use of concrete evidence (e.g., Ai Weiweiâs detention, quantitative detail on 1600 crafters) to support key analytical claims
Effective deployment of TOK terminology (e.g., âthe role of the knowerâ, âepistemic injusticeâ) demonstrating engagement with the conceptual framework
Diverse selection of objects (gallery art, social media artefact, political installation) illustrating how TOK themes manifest in varied contexts
weaknesses: Analysis occasionally drifts into descriptive narrative without tying back explicitly to the IA prompt
Some assertions (e.g., audience ownership diminishing artistic merit; viewers being âagitatedâ) lack supporting evidence or specificity
Discussion of the TikTok example trails off mid-thought, reducing coherence and analytical depth
Final concluding statements fail to restate the IA prompt explicitly, weakening overall cohesion
Opportunities remain to integrate citations more seamlessly into analysis and to pose an explicit knowledge question to frame the exhibition
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs exhibition clearly satisfies the requirements for the 7â8 band: each object is well contextualized and linked to the chosen prompt, with many claims supported by appropriate evidence and TOK concepts. However, intermittent lapses in precision, incomplete development of some arguments (notably around the TikTok artefact), and moments of descriptive rather than analytical commentary prevent the work from reaching the consistently strong justification expected in the 9â10 band. A score of 7 reflects solid performance across all strands while acknowledging these areas for refinement.

annotations.comment: The discussion ends mid-sentence, disrupting coherence; the student should complete the thought and reinforce how this example ties back to the IA prompt.
Insightful linkage between the installationâs visual effects and political critique establishes a convincing connection to power structures and knowledge control.
Quantitative detail about the 1600 crafters effectively supports the analysis of conformity, demonstrating evidence-based justification for the object.
The final concluding sentence generalizes power dynamics but omits an explicit restatement of the IA prompt, weakening the overall cohesion of the exhibition.
The student uses descriptive narrative effectively; shifting more consistently into TOK analysisâperhaps by referencing specific Ways of Knowingâwould deepen critical engagement.
Integrating your citations into the analytical discussion (rather than in separate sentences) will strengthen the support for your interpretive claims.
Overall, the studentâs selection of three diverse objects (gallery art, social media, political installation) effectively demonstrates how TOK themes manifest in varied real-world contexts.
The student clearly identifies the first object and situates it in a specific real-world context (Tate Modern, 2006), satisfying the requirement to link artefacts to concrete settings.
This paragraph reads as descriptive summary rather than analysis; explicitly linking the discussion back to the IA prompt (âWho owns knowledge?â) here would sharpen the focus on TOK implications.
Effective use of core TOK terminology (e.g., âthe role of the knowerâ) demonstrates engagement with the conceptual framework and begins to justify the artefactâs contribution to the exhibition.
Detailed contextual information about the neon signâs exhibition history strengthens the connection between the object and its broader cultural and historical significance.
The claim that the artworkâs meaning âemerges through interactionâ would benefit from concrete examples of audience interpretations to illustrate how spectators actively co-create knowledge.
Inclusion of a direct quote from the artist provides a strong primary-source perspective, enriching the justification for the objectâs role in examining knowledge ownership.
The second object is clearly introduced with specific details (TikTok video, Easter 2024, artist name), fulfilling the criterion of precise real-world context identification.
The assertion that viewers were âagitatedâ lacks supporting evidence; citing specific audience reactions or reliable sources would substantiate the claim of controversy.
This concluding statement on Object 2 is too brief to justify its inclusion; expanding on the implications for âWho owns knowledge?â would solidify the objectâs contribution.
General statements about relativism and artistic merit would be more convincing if illustrated with concrete examples of how subjective preferences overshadow deeper critique.
The third object is precisely named and contextualized, referencing Tate Modern and Ai Weiwei, which clearly establishes its real-world grounding.
The symbolism of sunflower seeds is well-noted but could be more powerfully tied to the prompt by explicitly considering how cultural symbols shape collective knowledge ownership.
Use of concrete historical facts about Ai Weiweiâs detention and censorship powerfully illustrates epistemic injustice in practice.
Appropriate deployment of the term âepistemic injusticeâ shows strong engagement with TOK concepts; integrating a brief definition could further strengthen clarity.
Consider adding an introductory paragraph that frames all three objects under the question âWho owns knowledge?â, enhancing coherence and guiding the readerâs focus.
Be careful to finish every paragraph fullyâcomplete arguments deliver clarity and prevent readers from missing key analytical connections.
To elevate this section, explicitly pose a knowledge question (e.g., âTo what extent do gatekeepers shape what knowledge is available?â) to frame the analysis.
The choice of a social-media artefact broadens the scope of the exhibition; ensure that you consistently tie its significance back to knowledge ownership rather than platform dynamics.
References are comprehensive; consider integrating footnote numbers into the text body to maintain flow and to remind readers of source support for each claim.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
